title: Reading good but theft attempt
content:
I paid for a romance and palm reading it said I could pay what I felt was right. I only had $1 in my account so that is what I paid. A week later they tried to charge me $49.99 without consent. Because of this that card/account is locked until they send me a new card. I used this card so I could respond fast to attempts like this. It isn’t a huge problem but they did attempt to take what was not theirs. There was no money left on the card so they couldn’t take anything. 

I tried to contact their costumer service but they don’t respond. I did not sign up for any subscription and when asked when I was doing the reading I said no but they tried anyway.  

The readings were okay but if you do this, use a prepaid card so they can’t take more than you want. You need to ensure your control on the money you use.

Update: As you see someone responded. As you can see they are gaslighting. Again I tried to contact customer service and three days later this is the only response. Also, I read it thoroughly it said it would only charge me, but I felt comfortable with and I put that I was only comfortable with one dollar. I don’t just feel I can’t trust you guys I know I can’t trust you guys. Just based on the reviews on here you have done this to hundreds of people. It is not safe to use your service unless they use a prepaid card because you will always charge what was not agreed on.

title: Such Bad Experience. Coast Me —$$$$
content:
My experience has been nothing short of infuriating and deeply disappointing. After receiving ONE share in SPCX IPO. All transactions I made was through bank accounts they themselves verified. Later, my account was suddenly restricted without explanation. For two weeks, I was locked out of basic functions, unable to trade, withdraw, or even understand what was happening. Then, without any justification, they escalated the situation and closed my account entirely, again refusing to provide a reason. Customer service was practically nonexistent. There is no way to call anyone, no way to speak to a human being, and no way to get timely help. The only option was messaging support, and every reply took two full days, only to repeat the same useless line: “ No  Update Yet, Your account is under review.”. It felt like talking to a wall. No transparency, no accountability, no effort to resolve anything.
I complied with every single request they made. I submitted everything they asked for:  A live selfie ,Government ID  & Three separate bank statements . None of it mattered. They ignored the documents, ignored the facts, and ignored the situation entirely. Even after providing everything, they still refused to tell me what “recent activity” supposedly triggered the restriction. To this day, I have never been told what I supposedly did wrong — because I didn’t do anything wrong. 
To make matters worse,they liquidate my assets with a GREAT Loss.
Then, while my account was restricted and unusable, Robinhood charged me $50 for an annual membership I never subscribed to. Charging a fee during a restriction — and for a service I didn’t even sign up for — is beyond unacceptable. It’s predatory.
The entire experience has shown me how little Robinhood values its customers. They shut down accounts without explanation, block access to funds, provide no real support, and hide behind vague automated messages. When a financial platform controls your money, trust is everything — and Robinhood has proven they don’t deserve any.
I went in expecting a modern, reliable trading platform. What I got instead was a nightmare: zero communication, zero transparency, and zero respect for the people who use their service. If you’re considering Robinhood, think twice. When something goes wrong — even if it’s their mistake — you will be left completely powerless. I would never recommend Robinhood to anyone.

title: DIDNT LET ME WITHDRAW MY MONEY
content:
If you do not want your money to be held hostage at the expense of your biometric data, do NOT use robinhood. 

Robinhood forced me to take a ‘selfie’ to take my money out. Their customer service team said its because they needed to verify my identity.

Well guess what? I verified my phone number, my address, my email, my bank accounts with plaid, my cell phone device, and I uploaded photos of my ID. What more verification could they possibly need? 

Their customer service team told me: “oh, the system automatically chooses who needs to take a selfie for verification purposes. We have no way of overriding it. You will not be able to take your money out without providing a selfie.”

I got this answer after trying to withdraw to THE SAME BANK ACCOUNT I USED TO DEPOSIT.

The disclaimer on the selfie says “By taking this picture…You also consent to Persona, our third party vendor, collecting and using your biometric data on our behalf to verify your identity, including through automated means”

Wow. Absolutely not. I have to consent to my biometric data being sold off? And if I don’t, I won’t be able to get my money? This sucks.

But in the end, i had to do it. Or else I wouldn’t have been able to get my money out. By the way, I didn’t give robinhood permission to use my camera on my phone but it still managed to take a scan of my face. My phone is an older model without faceID. Riddle me that.

I feel extorted for my data. Never recommending Robinhood to anyone. I didn’t consent to having my biometrics given to a third party, but I was forced to in the end. Really messed up imo. 

Don’t use this app, people. Go to a REAL BROKER like schwab or fidelity.

If you value convenience and don’t care about your privacy, go ahead and use this app. But if you like privacy and confidentiality, don’t even download it.
I hate money

title: Beware of hidden fees -
content:
Update - The refund was received 3 days after canceling the “upgrade”. I will no longer be using Robinhood due to the fact that this is an ongoing problem, according to others online, and should not be a recurring issue for inexperienced users. Because I wasn’t ready to do the “upgrade” due to financial reasons, I am now told I will never be able to have that option. I would have raised my star rating, but considering that this should’ve never happened, I’m leaving it as one star. I’m not saying the service is not worth having or that it’s not a reliable way to trade. For me, it’s the principle of the thing and something I can’t overlook.
——————-
Original review ———
A warning to others: If you decide to open a checking or savings account with Robinhood, they will automatically deduct $50 from your funds for an 'upgrade' to an annual fee (Robinhood Gold). I've since learned this is a widely reported trap that catches many users off guard because it is not made clear during the sign-up process. I closed the accounts immediately. Support told me to expect a refund in 3 days, and I’ll update this review if and when my money is actually returned. This shady practice instantly broke my trust, and I will no longer use their platform.

title: App UX. Add logos
content:
I’ve had a great overall experience using the app—it's reliable, easy to navigate, and I trust it for managing my investments. However, one feature that would significantly improve the user experience is adding company logos for investments.

Apps like Fidelity display company logos next to stocks and funds, which makes it much easier to quickly identify holdings at a glance. Right now, Robinhoods interface feels a bit too plain and requires more effort to visually scan through positions.

Adding logos would make the app feel more modern, intuitive, and user-friendly—especially for investors who are actively monitoring multiple positions. I hope this is something the development team considers in a future update.

title: Best in class
content:
I’ve been a loyal Robinhood customer for several years now, and I couldn’t be happier with the platform. From the very first day I signed up, Robinhood has made investing accessible, intuitive, and genuinely exciting. The clean interface, real-time data, seamless mobile experience, and thoughtful features like fractional shares and easy order management have turned me into a long-term advocate.
What really stands out is how reliable and user-friendly the app is during big moments. Just recently, I was thrilled to finally get my SpaceX share order executed through Robinhood. As someone who’s followed SpaceX’s incredible journey and innovation under Elon Musk, being able to participate as an investor feels like a milestone. Robinhood handled the process smoothly and efficiently—no unnecessary delays or complications. It executed exactly as hoped, and I’m genuinely excited to hold this piece of a company that’s pushing the boundaries of space exploration and technology.
Robinhood continues to impress me with its commitment to making advanced investing tools available to everyday people without high fees or barriers. Whether I’m checking market news, setting up alerts, reviewing my portfolio performance, or placing trades on the go, everything just works. The educational resources, community insights, and overall reliability have helped me stay informed and confident in my decisions over the years.
If you’re looking for an excellent trading platform that delivers on its promises, I highly recommend Robinhood. It’s more than just an app—it’s a trusted partner for building long-term wealth. Five stars all the way! I’m proud to keep growing my investments here and look forward to many more successful executions in the future. 🚀

content: I am a 54 year old retired military member who has had an extensive credit history for the past 30 years and an excellent rating for credit score.  I tried to establish an account only to run into the app requesting a photo of my physical SSN card.  Never in my 54 years of living have I ever been asked for a photo of my SSN card to open an account.  I know my SSN and i have provided it in everything required to open an account or to get hired for a job.  The reason I mentioned my age, military service and extensive credit history is because you are ONLY supposed to get asked for a physical card if you are young and do not have a credit history period or very little.  If I had my card available, I probably wouldn’t be complaining, but things like this occur when some snot nose grad student goes into a meeting and proposes how we can make our process more secure by not accomplishing an absolute thing other than enraging new customers.  A picture of an SSN card does nothing for security.  In fact it is by far the easiest form of ID to replicate.  I wonder how many future customers this company has lost due to this complete unnecessary step in order to create an account.  Way to go guys!  Un****real!

title: I love this app !!
content:
I’ve been using Duolingo to learn Portuguese because my brother started learning French so I thought I’d give it a try, and it’s honestly been an awesome experience. Since I already speak Spanish, I could recognize a lot of Portuguese words at first, but this app helped me go way beyond just recognizing similarities. It taught me vocabulary, pronunciation, grammar, and how to actually put sentences together.

One of the coolest things is that I’ll randomly hear people speaking Portuguese in videos, music, or out in public, and I’ll realize that I actually understand what they’re saying. I’ve even gotten to the point where I can have simple conversations in Portuguese, which is something I never expected when I first started.

The lessons are fun, easy to stick with, and make learning feel like a game instead of a chore. If you’re thinking about learning a new language, I genuinely recommend giving Duolingo a try. It’s helped me make real progress, and I’m excited to keep learning.

title: Duolingo is getting annoying.
content:
(PLEASE READ!)

I’m not being paid for this

I’m going off with the bad stuff first. 

I DONT LIKE STRANGERS FOLLOWING ME!!! It’s creepy. Same with my feed, these random people, not even on Duo staff, pop up and want me to like their achievement. I don’t care that you have 3 days for a friendstreak. They have hundreds of thousands of likes and they keep popping up. I suggest this be changed!!!

Now I’ll be positive. 

Ive been learning German on Duolingo for over a year now (current 700+ days!) and it’s been extremely useful. I was able to figure out what a family was saying in German whilst I was on a vacation, and can easily translate basic phrases to my friends/family. Other people say the German course is wrong with the dialects and everything, but I say it’s pretty great. I totally recommend it to anyone looking to learn math, Spanish, English, and/or German.

 Chess, is another story. Yes ive learned a lot in the past 3 months but ELO barely goes up if you win against Oscar, but if “Chess Daddy” (a recent phrase theyve decided would be another good addition that was added) beats YOU, the ELO goes down IMMENSELY. 

If you want to learn chess, you need to have patience. 

Super Duolingo/ Duolingo Max IS EXPENSIVE; but also worth it as you can do really anything with it. 

THANK YOU! ☘️

title: So great! Until the updates…
content: Please read to the end, I know it’s long. Duolingo is a great way for kids to learn a language most of the time. Yeah, the ads for Super are so annoying if you don’t have it, and it also kicks you out if you “run out of energy”, but overall, it was working well for me. The first real problem I had with it was that it deleted my 300 day streak and reset it to zero whenever the app wasn’t working for a few days. It wouldn’t let me do a lesson, so since I couldn’t do it for a little bit, it restarted my streak, which I had been working towards for A YEAR. Then, more recently,  it updated the German course and set my progress to a way more advanced level than I was at. It moved me up about 10 units. And since you can’t go back in Duolingo, only review, which doesn’t reteach you the words it only reviews, now I have to delete my entire course and reset it to my level, which deletes all my previous achievements in that language. For Duolingo creators, a great idea! But stop pushing Super Duolingo, and dont update courses if you can’t set back your progress to where it was. Also, leave a better way for users to contact you, because that will help with these random glitches. For parents and kids, a great app, just not for serious learners, unless you are also learning it in another way, like in school.

title: Course content updates are not well done
content: Honest review: I’ve used the app daily for over two months to learn German. I’ve learned about 500 words and progressed through 30 units. It has gotten difficult but every unit builds on the next….. until now. An update went through and the previous course material has all been rewritten with different content and different vocabulary. Where I’m struggling is that the app doesn’t help you identify what you now don’t know. Many of the words I’ve spent a lot of time practicing are now gone from my practice and they are replaced with hundreds of new words I’m not familiar with. That’s not a big deal in and of itself but the app doesn’t offer a way for you to effectively learn the content it thinks I now should already know and the current lessons assume I know those several hundred words and phrases. I don’t know how often these updates happen (because the app doesn’t tell you anything about it) but if this is a regular thing I would not invest much effort into this app. Maybe use of for free casually but focus on a more established program. If I could give the app advice I would say that it needs to have a better bridging strategy for its users when they rework all of the courses. Too bad, can be fun though

title: It’s pretty great
content:
Please add/fix
1. A way to search the ‘words’ section by word or translation 
2. Something to spend gems on that’s actually worth it (maybe bring back the duo costumes, I dunno) 
3. Mini games later in the path, and more detailed, longer, and more interesting ones 
4. Way to select difficulty of ai in chess 
5. Revert the icons on the path to the old version with the little animated icons. Please. Please. They were so much better. 
6. The chests appear way too frequently, to the point where it’s rare to not have an xp boost. Every time I finish a lesson, I get like 4 chests projectile vomited at me. Also, they contain MORE. USELESS. GEMS.
7. Something to buy with the gems. 
8. Please bring back the audio lessons; they were a great way to learn and had interesting stories 

Update: new unit tests absolutely SUCK, they are impossible and do not tell you the “correct” answer until it’s too late. Completely unclear how much you have to get right and what the heck you’re doing. Please revert back to the old system. It was much better there. 

Old review: 
This is a great app and works great if you want to learn a language. 

Please make the games more like games - they do not feel very challenging or engaging.

Lastly, certain exercises (no disternable pattern as to which) do not show up on the classroom dashboard. I sometimes have to do more than the required 3 in order for 3 to show up. Please fix, it’s super annoying.

Update: just got to the end of lesson screen and there was none of the like, “you got this much % right” or “took this long”, nor a continue button. Then the app crashed. Still not sure if it logged as a completed exercise, even though on the path it shows as done.

title: Too many taps, no plan to integrate progress into updates
content:
I’ve used this app a few times to learn survival basics for various countries and visiting. Recently I took on Japanese as I have a number of clients in Japan, who I’d like to have some polite basics with. 

My main complaint with the app is that the “Gamified learning” features (points, gems, chests, avatar outfits, etc…) have no real payoff/use, and therefore the reward system is entirely ineffective- which means I simply have to tap through 10 to 15 pointless screens after each lesson. Slowing progress. Either make the points do something actually fun like pay for more interesting lessons or get rid of them.

My next complaint is the updates system. I understand and actually appreciate that Duolingo updates their courses to match curricular standards- but they don’t do anything to bridge users from old versions of the curriculum to new ones. So suddenly I am given a lesson with entirely new words and kanji with no immediate resources to comprehend. Basically I’ll have to go and repeat several units. Which is frustrating because it means I’ll have to tap through all of those rewards screens again.

If you want to make learning fun, Do better.

title: AI Drivel
content: I hate the way Duolingo is structured.  I feel like I don’t really learn anything helpful.  They just toss grammar and vocabulary at you in no particular order, with no proper explanations, and hope you figure things out by making mistakes.  It is a frustrating way to learn.  I dropped off my family plan because my mother upgraded us to Max without consulting the rest of us.  It forces us to do the roleplays and video calls in order to get the XP bonuses, which I hate.  The conversations are annoying and the AI makes so many mistakes on picking up what I say, even when I try to speak clearly.  I have dropped down to Super on my own, but I may honestly just drop Duolingo entirely.  It isn’t fun for me and I feel like I have learned very little that is useful. title: Not a good match for me
content:
I wanted to like Duolingo, and I did at first. However, after using it for about three weeks, I became increasingly frustrated.

The biggest issue for me is that when I need extra help with pronunciation, the app doesn’t always provide enough opportunities to hear the words repeated. On the other hand, it spends a lot of time repeating extremely simple words like “New York” or “cake,” which I already know well. I often felt like my time wasn’t being used efficiently.

The lessons seem designed to encourage learners through repetition, and I understand that this approach works for many people. However, I found the learning experience confusing at times because it didn’t focus enough on the areas where I personally needed more practice.

This may simply be that Duolingo’s teaching style isn’t the best fit for my learning style. It’s a good app with a great concept, but unfortunately, it wasn’t the right match for me.

title: Different plans
content:
We didn’t know that there were different plans when we signed up. We’re in a contract for one year. And it’s pretty useless using this app unless you have the whole super plan because you cannot learn grammar and without grammar, you cannot read or write correctly. All this teaches you is words. Not how to form them.  The app is pretty worthless unless you have the full plan. I was disappointed because when we signed up I thought we got the AI conversation to learn how to speak to someone & it didn’t have that either. 

Another issue would be that it will give you  XP’s and it’ll ask you if you want to collect them later and it goes into the save for later and then if you go back to get it sometimes they’re not there. 

The same thing happens with the chests for time.  When you open your chest and it will say you win 10 minutes. Another 10 minutes. 15 minutes. all that should be added up for your study time and they’ll give you maybe 10 minutes out of all of that time. 

The game has glitches. I’ve reached out a couple times for help. Not fixed. 

Also this app is not user friendly for the visually impaired that have to use voiceover.  It speaks the same time as the VO. It needs to give time for the VO to speak before Duo speaks. And Due doesn’t. So you can’t hear it. And then it’s really hard to zoom in and do all the words.  It’s takes way to much time to use with VO. This app is not visually impaired friendly & needs updates.
